steps.title: Trace the loanword path
steps.content: The passage traces tomato from Nahuatl to Spanish to English and maize from Taíno to Spanish to English. In both examples, Spanish is the language through which Indigenous words entered English.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A invents Spanish contributions to Indigenous languages. Choice B compares vocabulary amounts the passage never quantifies. Choice C claims equal numbers of Nahuatl and Taíno words without evidence. Spanish is presented as the medium between Indigenous languages and English. The answer is D.
